channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Who Could Take Over for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei?
item.description: Now that Iran has declared that its supreme leader is dead, it is unclear which of his possible successors might rise.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times, Ebrahim Noroozi/Associated Press

item.title: ‘It’s Surreal’: Iranians in Disbelief After Supreme Leader Is Killed
item.description: Large crowds of people celebrated the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in American and Israeli strikes, while many others gathered to mourn.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Kiana Hayeri
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally to mourn Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader of Iran, in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: After Attack on Iran by the U.S. and Israel, See Photos and Videos From the Middle East
item.description: Here are images from Iran, Israel and elsewhere in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians in Tehran, the capital, mourned Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the country’s supreme leader, on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Attacks Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Qatar and Bahrain After U.S.-Israeli Strikes
item.description: More than 100 people were injured and at least four were killed after Iran fired hundreds of missiles and drones at Gulf cities, including Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Doha.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim and Omnia Al Desoukie
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Altaf Qadri/Associated Press
item.media:description: A plume of smoke from an Iranian strike rising in the distance from the Dubai International Airport.

item.title: The U.S. Released the Epstein Files. The Arrests Are Overseas.
item.description: While the latest tranche of documents about the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has exposed a deeply American scandal, it is Europe where the aggressive enforcement is taking place.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Chris Radburn/Reuters
item.media:description: Police officers at the entrance to Wood Farm on the Sandringham Estate in Norfolk, Britain, last week, where Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor is currently based.

item.title: In Ukraine, a Community of ‘Simple Believers’ Shuns the Modern World
item.description: The Christians known as viruiuchi prostaky see electricity, cars, higher education and much else as distractions from what really matters.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Lima and Yurii Shyvala
item.media:description: Laying a foundation for a house in the village of Kosmyryn, in western Ukraine, in November.

item.title: The Bloody Rise and Fall of Mexico’s Top Crime Boss
item.description: El Mencho’s brutality and business acumen put him atop the cartel world, until he made a fatal mistake.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas, Paulina Villegas and Maria Abi-Habib
item.media:credit: Cesar Rodriguez for The New York Times
item.media:description: After Mexican forces killed a notorious cartel kingpin on Sunday, his followers set fire to hundreds of vehicles, like this one beside a highway in Jalisco state.

item.title: Trump Gives Green Light to Private Oil Sales to Cuba
item.description: After blocking foreign oil shipments, the Trump administration is now allowing small amounts of oil to enter Cuba — as long as they circumvent government hands.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles and David C. Adams
item.media:credit: Yamil Lage/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The Nico Lopez oil refinery in Havana this month.

item.title: Carney Visiting India, Australia and Japan to Build Canada’s ‘Middle Power’ Bonds
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney visits India, Australia and Japan seeking deals to strengthen his country’s links to Indo-Pacific powers and break Canada’s dependence on the United States.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff and Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada, with his wife, Diana Fox Carney, arriving in Mumbai, India, on Friday.

item.title: Taiwan Arms Sale Approved by Congress Is Delayed as Trump Plans Visit to Beijing
item.description: The package worth billions of dollars and endorsed by lawmakers is stalled at the State Department as the U.S. and China plan an April summit.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong and Robert Jimison
item.media:credit: I-Hwa Cheng/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An Air Force Patriot missile system was deployed at a local park during Taiwan’s annual Han Kuang military exercise last year.

item.title: Pakistan Strikes Afghanistan in ‘Open War’ Against Taliban Regime
item.description: The airstrikes came hours after Afghan troops had attacked Pakistani border positions and follow months of worsening relations between the neighboring countries.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Safiullah Padshah and Zia ur-Rehman
item.media:credit: Wahidullah Kakar/Associated Press
item.media:description: Taliban soldiers looking toward Pakistan from the Afghan side of the Torkham border crossing, on Friday.

item.title: A Trump Call Ignited Saudi-U.A.E. Feud
item.description: A request made to President Trump about the war in Sudan is at the heart of a diplomatic dispute between Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman of Saudi Arabia at an investment forum in Washington in November.

item.title: Some Russians Go Without Heat or Power in Winter, as Energy System Falls Into Disrepair
item.description: A dozen major blackouts and central heating cutoffs across the country have affected hundreds of thousands during a brutal winter.
item.dc:creator: Nataliya Vasilyeva
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: Murmansk, Russia, in 2023. The city has been plagued by power and heating outages this winter.

item.title: Were Duterte’s Speeches Orders to Kill or Hyperbole?
item.description: Judges at the International Criminal Court have heard starkly different interpretations this week of the words of former President Rodrigo Duterte of the Philippines.
item.dc:creator: Aie Balagtas See
item.media:credit: Simon Wohlfahrt/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Outside the International Criminal Court this week. Hearings have drawn critics and supporters of Rodrigo Duterte, who as president of the Philippines ordered a brutal crackdown on drugs.

item.title: Hungary Plays Spoiler in Europe as Orban Strains for Votes at Home
item.description: Facing a serious election challenge, Prime Minister Viktor Orban is holding up a big E.U. loan for Ukraine. Analysts say the timing is no coincidence.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.media:credit: Attila Kisbenedek/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban of Hungary in Budapest last month.

item.title: Bagram Air Base Targeted by Pakistan, Afghanistan Says
item.description: The Afghan military said it had thwarted a Pakistani airstrike on the former U.S. base, Bagram airfield, amid an intensifying campaign that has targeted dozens of military sites across the country.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Safiullah Padshah
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder for The New York Times
item.media:description: Taliban fighters at the Bagram air field in Afghanistan in 2021.

item.title: China’s Parents Are Outsourcing the Homework Grind to A.I.
item.description: Parents in China are turning to A.I. chatbots and other tools to help their children gain an edge and ease the fighting over homework.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang and Jiawei Wang

item.title: China’s New Study Buddy: The A.I. Chatbot
item.description: Parents in China are using homegrown chatbots like DeepSeek and Doubao as well as Google’s Gemini to help their children with homework and give them an academic edge.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

item.title: Iran Strikes U.S. Military Facilities in Dubai, Bahrain and Other Middle East Locations
item.description: A Times analysis of satellite imagery and verified videos shows Iran found vulnerabilities in U.S. locations in the region.
item.dc:creator: Devon Lum and Haley Willis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut After Hezbollah Attacks
item.description: Residents fled in panic as Israeli jets struck Hezbollah strongholds in response to a midnight barrage, an expansion of the war between Israel, the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Hussein Malla/Associated Press
item.media:description: Smoke rising in Beirut, Lebanon, early on Monday.

item.title: Trump Says Iran War Could Last Weeks and Gives Competing Visions of New Regime
item.description: In a brief interview, he said the country’s hardened military should simply surrender their weapons to the Iranian public.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump boarding Air Force One at Palm Beach International Airport in West Palm Beach, Fla., on Sunday. He spoke to The Times in a brief phone interview.

item.title: In New York, Fury, Anxiety and Joy Over the Attacks on Iran
item.description: As Mayor Zohran Mamdani assailed what he called a “catastrophic escalation” in Iran, some Iranian Americans worried about what comes next, while others celebrated.
item.dc:creator: Dodai Stewart
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York said Americans did not support a “war of aggression” in Iran.

item.title: Iranian Strikes on Gulf States Reach the Hundreds
item.description: Iran has fired nearly 400 missiles and over 800 drones across the Persian Gulf in the past two days, according to government reports.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Hamad I Mohammed/Reuters
item.media:description: A damaged building in Manama, Bahrain, on Sunday.

item.title: Strike on Girls’ School Kills 115, Iranian State Media Says
item.description: Videos and images verified by The New York Times showed that at least half of the school was destroyed. It was not immediately clear why the school was hit, or which country’s forces had fired at it.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne, Ephrat Livni and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: In Turn From Iran Talks to War, U.S. Casts Doubt on Diplomacy
item.description: President Trump again deployed heavy force against a country with which he had been negotiating, continuing a pattern seen in previous attacks on Iran and Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump, seen on Friday, has given little public explanation on why he cut off talks and attacked Iran.

item.title: What Now in Iran?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead. Whoever takes his place will shape the Middle East’s future.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: How the Assault on Iran Unfolded
item.description: Despite warnings after an earlier wave of killings, top Iranian officials gathered in person, and Israel seized the chance to kill Iran’s supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally to mourn the supreme leader, Ayatollah Khamenei in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Iran’s Ballistic Missile Launchers Have Been Cut by Half, Israeli Official Says
item.description: The Israeli military says it identified a significant recent acceleration in Iran’s efforts to rebuild its missile production capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Gabby Sobelman
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A missile on display in Tehran in October.

item.title: Stakes Quickly Rise for U.S. in Iran War, With 3 Service Members Killed
item.description: The grim toll signaled that Iran was more prepared for war than the Trump administration anticipated, U.S. military officials said.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Smoke rose on Sunday near Erbil International Airport, which hosts U.S.-led coalition troops in Iraq’s autonomous Kurdistan region.

item.title: Strikes Destroyed Newly Built Structures at Khamenei’s Compound
item.description: Images showed new roofs and structures connecting existing buildings at the compound where Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Democrats Question Trump’s Urgency to Attack Iran
item.description: Lawmakers questioned whether the United States faced an “imminent” threat from Iran, a requirement for the president to lawfully initiate military action without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: The War Powers Act requires the president to consult with Congress “in every possible instance” before taking military action abroad, and it limits the unilateral use of force to 60 days.

item.title: What’s Next for Iran? Big Change Seems Certain, but Direction Is Unclear.
item.description: Experts say that Iran’s clerical rulers may be too deeply entrenched for Iranians to topple them, and that the U.S. and Israeli strikes risk setting off deeper radicalization or violence.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians mourning their supreme leader at a rally in Tehran.

item.title: NYPD Steps Up Security After U.S.-Israel Attacks in Iran
item.description: Officers in the Police Department’s international liaison unit, including at outposts abroad, are gathering information about potential threats to New York City.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rebecca Weiner, deputy commissioner of intelligence and counterterrorism for the Police Department, said the city was in a heightened threat environment.

item.title: Trump Says He’s Open to Talks With Iran in Interview With The Atlantic
item.description: In an interview with The Atlantic, President Trump said the country’s new leaders after the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei “want to talk” but did not say whom he was referring to.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has kept a low profile since the United States and Israel struck Iran, except to call some individual reporters.

item.title: Maersk to Halt Some Red Sea Shipping in Sign of War’s Disruption to Global Supply Chain
item.description: Though the conflict is centered on the Persian Gulf, shipping companies fear that the Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen could resume attacks on vessels in the Red Sea hundreds of miles to the west.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Martin Bernetti/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Maersk, a large shipping company, said on Sunday that it would stop sending some of its vessels through the Red Sea.

item.title: Iran Fires Cheap Drones Into Arab Countries, Wreaking Havoc
item.description: It already proved its effectiveness on the battlefields of Ukraine. Now the Iranian-made Shahed-136 kamikaze has been unleashed across the Arab Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Sanjana Varghese, Adam Rasgon and Dmitriy Khavin

item.title: Where the Strikes Leave Iranians
item.description: Iran is reeling a day after the United States and Israel carried out a large attack that killed Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Erika Solomon, the Iran bureau chief for The New York Times, explains how ordinary Iranians are responding to the sudden end of Khamenei’s authoritarian rule and the uncertain future that lies ahead.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Christina Thornell, Leila Medina, Nikolay Nikolov and June Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran’s regional alliance has been severely weakened in recent years.
item.description: Weakened after two years of wars
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: A defaced banner depicting the ousted president, Bashar al-Assad, in Damascus, the Syrian capital, last year. One of the largest blows to Iran was the loss of Syria as an allied state after the Assad dictatorship fell in late 2024.

item.title: 9 Killed in Israeli City Near Jerusalem After Iranian Missile Strike
item.description: The toll in Beit Shemesh was the highest in Israel since the start of the conflict with Iran on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer, Isabel Kershner and Malachy Browne
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times

item.title: Protesters Try to Storm U.S. Consulate in Karachi, Pakistan
item.description: At least 22 people were killed in protests across Pakistan as Iran’s neighbors brace for turmoil and the fallout of a possible regional conflict.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Zia ur-Rehman and Yaqoob Akbary
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Air travel to and from the Middle East has become increasingly precarious.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:credit: Altaf Qadri/Associated Press
item.media:description: Passengers stranded by the closing of Dubai International Airport waited for help in a parking lot there on Sunday.

item.title: Pentagon Announces First Three U.S. Deaths in War With Iran
item.description: U.S. Central Command did not say where the troops were killed but added that five others had been seriously wounded and that several had shrapnel injuries.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump on Friday. He said that the campaign against Iran could result in American deaths.

item.title: Iran’s Regime May Survive, but the Middle East Will Be Changed
item.description: A badly weakened Iran will no longer intimidate or threaten its neighbors in the same way. The regional impact could be comparable to the collapse of the Soviet Union.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians mourning the killing of the supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, on Sunday.

item.title: Israelis Are Tense but Relieved That Iran’s Supreme Leader Is Dead
item.description: Iranian missile and drone attacks have caused anxiety across Israel, but many believe it is a price worth paying for killing Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: People taking shelter in Herzliya, Israel, on Sunday.

item.title: OPEC Plus to Boost Oil Production as Iran Strikes Threaten Price Spike
item.description: The cartel pledged to increase output by 206,000 barrels a day from next month, and said that members would “closely monitor and assess market conditions.”
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
item.media:credit: Ahmed Jadallah/Reuters
item.media:description: An oil refinery and oil terminal in Ras Tanura, Saudi Arabia. The eight oil-producing countries in the group known as OPEC Plus, which includes Saudi Arabia, said on Sunday that they would increase oil production in April.

item.title: The U.S.-Israeli attacks have killed a number of Iranian leaders.
item.description: The United States and Israel killed many senior Iranian leaders in the .
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, was killed in an Israeli strike on Saturday.

item.title: A top Iranian security official says an interim government will be formed on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top security official, who was close to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Why Diplomacy Was Doomed: Trump’s Issue Was Iran’s Leadership Itself
item.description: President Trump’s envoys negotiated with Iran over its nuclear program. But Mr. Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel wanted much more.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong and Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The “radical Shia clerics” leading Iran could not be trusted, Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in recent weeks.

item.title: Israel launches new military strikes as Iran retaliates after Khamenei’s killing.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: People taking shelter in Tel Aviv on Sunday as air-raid sirens warned of another Iranian missile and drone attack.

item.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Ayatollah Khamenei became Iran’s supreme leader in 1989, wielding ultimate religious and political authority over the state and security apparatus.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: A giant billboard featuring a portrait of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, in the center of Tehran’s Valiasr Square last year.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi, Helene Cooper, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Tyler Pager and Yan Zhuang
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: The C.I.A. Helped Pinpoint a Gathering of Iranian Leaders. Then Israel Struck.
item.description: The killing of Iran’s supreme leader and other top Iranian officials came after close intelligence sharing between the United States and Israel, according to people familiar with the operation.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Ronen Bergman, Eric Schmitt and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Airbus, via Reuters
item.media:description: A satellite image shows black smoke rising and heavy damage at the compound of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Trump Stays Out of Public View After U.S. Launches Military Assault on Iran
item.description: President Trump did not deliver a formal address to the American public to explain why the country was at war, a departure from his predecessors.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump arriving in Palm Beach, Fla., on Friday.

item.title: Iranians Take to Streets to Celebrate Khamenei’s Death
item.description: Some Iranians said on social media that they were privately mourning the supreme leader. But displays of exuberance broke out in cities across the country.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Christiaan Triebert
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Hard-Line Cleric Who Made Iran a Regional Power, Is Dead at 86
item.description: As Iran’s second supreme leader, he brutally crushed dissent at home and expanded Iran’s footprint abroad, challenging Saudi Arabia for regional dominance.
item.dc:creator: Alan Cowell and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who led for more than three decades, shaped Iran’s Islamic revolution far more than the Islamic Republic’s founder, Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini.

item.title: After Iran Attacks, Ship Traffic Plummets in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: One of the world’s most vital maritime arteries saw a 70 percent drop in vessel traffic.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Alexander Cardia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: False and outdated videos circulated online after the Iran strikes.
item.dc:creator: Stuart A. Thompson

item.title: See a Breakdown of Ayatollah Khamenei and Other Top Iranian Leaders
item.description: Among those killed in strikes, according to the U.S. and Israel, were Iran’s supreme leader and three of his top military commanders.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The attacks touched off protests across the United States and abroad.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn, Zia Haider Rahman and Robert Chiarito
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
item.media:description: A demonstration in Times Square in New York on Saturday was one of dozens of protests across the country and around the world opposing the joint U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran.

item.title: U.S. Ability to Determine What Comes Next in Iran Might Be Limited
item.description: Questions remain about how much effort the Trump administration will put into changing the Iranian government.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People demonstrating in support of the government in Tehran on Saturday.
